⏰ Total Time: 26 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 8

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 3 cups mixed fresh fruit (e.g., strawberries, mango, and apples), chopped
  • 1 tablespoon lemon juice, fresh
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract (optional)
  • 1/4 teaspoon ground cinnamon (optional)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Start by washing and chopping your chosen fresh fruit into small pieces. This helps the blender process them more evenly. Using ripe, sweet fruit is key here because the natural sugars will intensify when dried, so pick fruits that taste good fresh. Next, puree the fruit in a high-speed blender or food processor until completely smooth. If you see any chunks or fibers, keep blending. This smooth texture is essential for the fruit leather to dry evenly and have a pleasant chew. If you want a bit of zing, add the fresh lemon juice now, which brightens the flavor and helps preserve the color. Avoid adding sugar or thickeners, as they can affect drying time and texture. 10 minutes The puree should be completely smooth, thick but spreadable, without any lumps.
  2. 2 Line a large baking sheet or dehydrator tray with parchment paper or a silicone mat. Pour the fruit puree onto the lined tray and spread it evenly using an offset spatula or the back of a spoon. Aim for about 1/8-inch thickness to ensure it dries uniformly without becoming brittle. If it’s too thick, the drying will take much longer and the texture might be gummy in the middle. If it’s too thin, the leather can dry out too much and become brittle. Smooth the edges and surface carefully to avoid uneven spots. This step is crucial because uneven thickness causes uneven drying and texture problems later. 5 minutes The puree should form a smooth, even layer with no visible lumps or thick spots.
  3. 3 Set your oven to the lowest temperature possible, ideally around 140°F (60°C), or use a food dehydrator if you have one. Place the tray in the oven or dehydrator and let the fruit puree dry slowly. This slow drying is what transforms the puree into chewy, pliable fruit leather without cooking or caramelizing the sugars. Avoid higher temperatures as they can cause the fruit sugars to burn or the leather to become brittle. Depending on your oven and fruit choice, drying can take anywhere from 4 to 6 hours. Check every hour after 3 hours to avoid over-drying. 4-6 hours The fruit leather should be dry to the touch, no longer sticky, and peel away easily from the parchment.
  4. 4 Once the fruit leather is dry but still pliable, carefully peel it off the parchment paper. If it sticks, gently loosen edges with a spatula or let it dry a bit longer. Then, cut the leather into strips or desired shapes using kitchen scissors or a sharp knife. Roll each strip up with parchment paper to keep them from sticking together. These rolls store well and are easy to grab for a quick snack. If the leather feels too sticky or soft, it needs more drying time. If it’s cracking or breaking when rolled, it’s over-dried. 15 minutes Smooth, flexible sheets that peel easily and roll without cracking.
  5. 5 Store the fruit leather rolls in an airtight container or zip-top bag at room temperature. They’ll keep for up to 2 weeks, but I usually eat them faster because they’re so good! For longer storage, you can refrigerate or freeze them to maintain freshness. Avoid exposing them to moisture or heat, which can cause stickiness or spoilage. When freezing, wrap individual rolls tightly in parchment and then place in a freezer bag to prevent freezer burn. 5 minutes Stored properly, the fruit leather remains chewy and flavorful.
  6. 6 If you want to add a twist, try sprinkling cinnamon or adding vanilla extract to the puree before drying. These subtle flavors complement many fruits and make your leather taste a bit more special. Just be sure to mix well so the flavors distribute evenly. You can also experiment with different fruit combos like peach and raspberry or apple and pear. Just keep an eye on drying times as some fruits have more water content and may take longer. 5 minutes Even flavor distribution without clumps or streaks.
  7. 7 For a smoother texture, strain the fruit puree through a fine mesh sieve before spreading. This removes seeds or fibrous bits that might make the leather less pleasant to chew. This extra step is especially helpful when using fruits like raspberries or blackberries. It’s a bit more work but results in a silky texture that’s worth it if you’re picky about seeds. 10 minutes Puree passes through sieve easily with no seeds or lumps remaining.
  8. 8 If you don’t have a dehydrator or oven with a low setting, you can dry the fruit leather outside on a sunny day using a mesh drying rack covered with cheesecloth to keep bugs away. This traditional method takes longer and depends on weather but can produce lovely results with a slightly different texture. Just be sure to bring it inside at night to avoid moisture. 1-2 days depending on weather Leather is dry and pliable with no sticky spots.
  9. 9 Before serving, let the fruit leather come to room temperature if refrigerated or frozen. This restores its chewiness and flavor. If it feels too stiff, you can soften it slightly by wrapping it in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 10-15 seconds, but don’t overdo it or it will get sticky. Enjoy your naturally sweet, preservative-free snack anytime you need a healthy boost! 5 minutes Soft, chewy texture that’s easy to bite and roll.

item.description: Planning Easter meals can be overwhelming, but this simple menu guide makes it a breeze. Featuring fresh, seasonal ingredients and easy recipes that flow from brunch to dinner, it’s perfect for family gatherings. | ⏱️ Prep: 20min | Cook: 40min | Serves: 6 | #cat1761230422512 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 20 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 40 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 60 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 6 large eggs, room temperature
  • 1 cup whole milk
  • 1 teaspoon salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon black pepper
  • 1 tablespoon butter
  • 1 cup fresh spinach, chopped
  • 1/2 cup shredded cheddar cheese
  • 1/2 pound cooked ham, diced
  • 1 loaf crusty bread, sliced
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • 1 bunch asparagus, trimmed
  • 2 tablespoons lemon juice
  • Fresh herbs for garnish (parsley, chives)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Start by preheating your oven to 375°F (190°C). While it warms up, whisk together the eggs, milk, salt, and pepper in a large bowl until fully combined and slightly frothy. This aeration is key for a light, fluffy texture in the final dish. Avoid overwhisking to prevent tough eggs. Setting the oven now ensures it’s ready once you assemble the casserole. 10 minutes Egg mixture should look smooth and slightly bubbly when whisked properly
  2. 2 Grease a 9x13-inch baking dish with butter, then layer in the diced ham, chopped spinach, and shredded cheddar cheese evenly. Pour the egg mixture over the top, pressing down gently to submerge ingredients. This layering helps distribute flavors and textures evenly. If you skip this, the casserole might bake unevenly or dry out in spots. 5 minutes Egg mixture fully covers the fillings with no dry patches
  3. 3 Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for about 30-35 minutes. Keep an eye on it starting at 25 minutes — you want the top to be set and lightly golden but not browned. Overbaking will dry out the eggs and make the texture rubbery. A gentle jiggle in the center indicates it’s just done. 30-35 minutes Top is set, golden, and the center jiggles slightly when nudged
  4. 4 While the casserole bakes, prepare the asparagus. Toss trimmed spears with olive oil, salt, and pepper, then roast on a baking sheet in the oven alongside the casserole for 12-15 minutes, turning once halfway through. Roasting brings out natural sweetness and keeps them crisp-tender. Don’t overcrowd the pan or they’ll steam instead of roast. 15 minutes Asparagus is bright green with slightly browned tips and tender when pierced
  5. 5 Slice the crusty bread and lightly brush each slice with olive oil. Toast in the oven or on a skillet until golden and crisp. This adds a crunchy texture contrast to the creamy casserole and fresh asparagus. Avoid skipping this step or the bread might feel soggy next to the eggs. 8 minutes Bread slices are golden brown and crisp to the touch
  6. 6 Once the casserole is out of the oven, let it rest for 5 minutes to set up before slicing. This resting time helps the eggs finish cooking gently and makes serving cleaner. Cutting too soon can cause the casserole to fall apart. 5 minutes Casserole holds shape when sliced, no liquid pooling
  7. 7 Drizzle the roasted asparagus with fresh lemon juice and sprinkle chopped herbs over both the casserole and asparagus. This final touch brightens flavors and adds a fresh aroma that’s perfect for springtime meals. 2 minutes Visible fresh herbs and lemon sheen on vegetables
  8. 8 Plate the casserole slices with roasted asparagus and toasted bread. Serve immediately while warm. The combination of creamy eggs, savory ham, crisp bread, and zesty asparagus makes a balanced and satisfying meal from brunch through dinner. 3 minutes Food is warm, vibrant, and inviting on the plate
  9. 9 Clean up and enjoy! If you have leftovers, store them properly (see storage tips) and reheat gently to keep textures intact. This menu is great for making ahead and enjoying over a couple of days. 5 minutes Kitchen tidy, leftovers stored airtight

A tender sponge cake shaped perfectly and frosted with fluffy buttercream. Ideal for spring celebrations or family gatherings! | ⏱️ Prep: 40min | Cook: 50min | Serves: 8 | #cat1761229712965 #Mediumpost #post #cooking #foodie
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 40 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 50 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 90 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 8

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 2 cups all-purpose flour, sifted
  • 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar
  • 4 large eggs, room temperature
  • 1 cup unsalted butter, softened
  • 1 cup whole milk, room temperature
  • 2 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1 tablespoon vanilla extract
  • Buttercream frosting: 1 cup unsalted butter, softened
  • 4 cups powdered sugar, sifted
  • 2 teaspoons vanilla extract
  • 2-4 tablespoons milk or cream
  • Food coloring (optional, for decoration)
  • Decorative eyes and nose candies

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease your lamb-shaped cake mold thoroughly with butter, then lightly dust with flour. This step ensures the cake releases easily without sticking or breaking apart. Proper greasing is crucial because the intricate shape of the mold can trap batter in corners, so take your time to cover every nook. Avoid using too much flour, as it can create a dry crust on the cake’s surface. If you don’t have a lamb mold, a bundt pan or loaf pan can be used, but the charming shape will be lost. 10 minutes The mold feels evenly greased with no dry spots; a light dusting of flour clings uniformly.
  2. 2 In a large bowl, cream the softened butter and granulated sugar together using an electric mixer on medium speed until the mixture is pale and fluffy—about 5 minutes. This aeration step is key to a light cake texture because it traps tiny air bubbles that expand during baking. If you rush this or skip it, the cake will be dense and heavy. Scrape down the sides of the bowl occasionally to ensure even mixing. 5 minutes Butter and sugar mixture looks lighter in color and has a fluffy, airy texture.
  3. 3 Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition to fully incorporate them. Adding eggs slowly helps maintain the batter’s emulsion and prevents it from curdling, which can cause a grainy texture. If your eggs are cold, they can cause the butter to seize, so make sure they’re at room temperature. If the batter looks curdled, don’t worry—it usually smooths out once the dry ingredients are added. 3 minutes Batter looks smooth and glossy after all eggs are incorporated.
  4. 4 In a separate bowl, whisk together the sifted flour, baking powder, and salt. Sifting aerates the flour and removes lumps, ensuring an even rise and tender crumb. Baking powder is your leavening agent here, so make sure it’s fresh for the best lift. Salt balances the sweetness and enhances flavor. 2 minutes Dry ingredients are evenly combined with no visible clumps.
  5. 5 Add the dry ingredients to the butter mixture alternately with the milk, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Mix gently on low speed just until combined after each addition to avoid overworking the batter, which can develop gluten and make the cake tough. The batter should be smooth but slightly thick. Stir in the vanilla extract last for a fragrant finish. 5 minutes Batter is smooth, thick but pourable, with no streaks of flour.
  6. 6 Pour the batter into the prepared lamb mold, smoothing the top with a spatula. Tap the mold gently on the counter to release any large air bubbles that might cause holes or tunnels in the cake. This step is important to ensure an even crumb and a smooth surface for frosting. Avoid overfilling the mold to prevent batter spilling over during baking. 5 minutes Batter evenly fills the mold with a smooth surface and no large bubbles.
  7. 7 Bake the cake for 45-50 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the thickest part comes out clean. Ovens vary, so start checking at 40 minutes. Avoid opening the oven door too early, as temperature fluctuations can cause the cake to sink. Once done, let the cake cool in the mold for 15 minutes before gently turning it out onto a wire rack to cool completely. This resting prevents cracking and sticking. 50 minutes Toothpick inserted in center comes out clean; cake springs back when lightly pressed.
  8. 8 While the cake cools, prepare the buttercream frosting. Beat the softened butter on medium speed until creamy, then gradually add sifted powdered sugar, beating well after each addition. Add vanilla extract and milk or cream a tablespoon at a time until you reach a light, spreadable consistency. Whip on high speed for a few minutes to get a fluffy texture perfect for piping the lamb’s woolly coat. 15 minutes Frosting is smooth, light, and holds soft peaks.
  9. 9 Once the cake is completely cool, frost it with the buttercream using a spatula or piping bag fitted with a star tip to create the lamb’s fluffy texture. Take your time to cover all surfaces evenly. Add candy eyes and a small candy nose to bring the lamb to life. Chill the decorated cake for at least 30 minutes to set the frosting before serving. This helps the frosting firm up and makes slicing easier. 20 minutes Frosting is evenly piped with textured swirls; decorations are securely in place.

item.title: Whimsical Butterfly Peanut Butter Toast Art for a Playful Breakfast
item.description: This fun butterfly toast is perfect for brightening up breakfast or snack time. Using peanut butter as the canvas and fresh fruit for wings, it’s a creative way to get kids excited about eating. Easy and playful! | ⏱️ Prep: 10min | Cook: 5min | Serves: 2 | #cat1761230422512 #Easypost #quickpost #30minutemeals #post
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 10 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 5 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 15 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 2

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 2 slices whole wheat bread, toasted
  • 4 tablespoons creamy peanut butter, room temperature
  • 1 small banana, sliced
  • 6 strawberries, hulled and halved
  • 1 tablespoon honey (optional)
  • A handful of blueberries

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Start by toasting your bread slices until they’re golden and slightly crisp but still soft enough to spread peanut butter smoothly. Toasting is key here because it prevents sogginess when you add the peanut butter and fruit. Avoid burning the toast as it will overpower the mild flavors and make the texture unpleasant. Keep an eye on your toaster or pan to get that perfect light brown color, which also adds a nice crunch contrast. 5 minutes Toast should be golden brown with a crisp edge but not hard or blackened.
  2. 2 Spread about 2 tablespoons of creamy peanut butter evenly on each slice of toast. Using creamy peanut butter helps it spread smoothly without tearing the bread. The peanut butter acts as the butterfly’s body and the adhesive base for the fruit wings, so make sure the layer is thick enough to hold the toppings but not so thick it overwhelms the toast. 2 minutes Peanut butter layer should be smooth and even, covering the entire surface of the toast.
  3. 3 Arrange banana slices vertically down the center of each toast to form the butterfly’s body. Bananas are soft and sweet, complementing the peanut butter nicely. Be gentle when placing the slices to avoid breaking the bread or smudging the peanut butter. This central line gives the butterfly its recognizable shape. 3 minutes Banana slices should be neatly lined up in a straight column without gaps.
  4. 4 Next, create the butterfly wings by placing strawberry halves on each side of the banana body. Position the pointed ends facing outward to mimic wing tips. The strawberries add a vibrant red color and fresh tartness that balances the creamy peanut butter. Press them lightly so they stick but don’t squish the bread. 3 minutes Strawberry halves should be evenly spaced on both sides, creating a symmetrical wing shape.
  5. 5 Scatter a few blueberries around the wings to add contrast and extra pops of color. Blueberries also bring a juicy burst and subtle sweetness. This step is optional but really amps up the visual appeal. Place them carefully so they don’t roll off the toast. 1 minute Blueberries are nestled snugly among the strawberries without crowding.
  6. 6 Drizzle a light amount of honey over the butterfly to add a glossy finish and a touch of natural sweetness. Honey also helps seal the fruit in place slightly and makes the toast more visually appealing. Be careful not to overdo it, or the toast might become sticky and soggy. 1 minute Honey appears as a thin, shiny glaze over the fruit without pooling.
  7. 7 Optionally, add a few tiny mint leaves or edible flowers around the butterfly for a fresh aroma and extra color contrast. This elevates the presentation and makes the toast feel special, perfect for a weekend brunch or a kid’s party. 2 minutes Garnishes are fresh and vibrant, complementing the butterfly without overwhelming it.
  8. 8 Serve immediately to enjoy the crisp toast and fresh fruit textures. Peanut butter can make the bread soggy if left too long, so this is best eaten right away. If you need to prep ahead, toast and spread peanut butter but add fruit just before serving. 0 minutes Toast remains crisp and fruit looks fresh without moisture pooling.
  9. 9 Clean up any sticky drips on the plate or edges for a neat presentation. This little extra step makes the butterfly toast look even more appetizing and shows care in serving. Plus, it keeps the eating experience tidy, especially for kids. 1 minute Plate looks clean and inviting with no sticky smears.

item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 20 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 35 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 lb fresh asparagus, trimmed
  • 2 tablespoons olive oil
  • Zest of 1 lemon
  • 1 teaspoon sea salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
  • 1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese (optional)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). This high heat is key for roasting asparagus so it gets tender but also develops those delicious caramelized spots. Spread the trimmed asparagus in a single layer on a rimmed baking sheet to avoid steaming. If the spears are crowded, they’ll release moisture and steam instead of roasting, which dulls the flavor and texture. 10 minutes Asparagus looks vibrant green with slightly browned tips and a tender but firm texture when pierced with a fork.
  2. 2 Drizzle the asparagus with olive oil, then sprinkle with lemon zest, sea salt, and black pepper. Toss gently with your hands or tongs to coat evenly. The olive oil helps the seasonings stick and promotes browning, while the lemon zest adds fresh citrus aroma that brightens the dish. Be sure to use fresh lemon zest, not dried, for the best flavor. 5 minutes Spears are evenly coated, glistening but not swimming in oil.
  3. 3 Place the baking sheet in the oven and roast for 12-15 minutes, shaking the pan or turning the asparagus halfway through. This ensures even cooking and browning. Keep an eye on the tips so they don’t burn—roasted asparagus should be tender with a slight crispness, not mushy or charred. 12-15 minutes Asparagus is tender when pierced but still has a slight snap, with golden brown spots on the tips.
  4. 4 Remove from the oven and immediately sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese while still hot, if using. The residual heat will slightly melt the cheese, adding a savory richness that complements the lemon and asparagus perfectly. 2 minutes Parmesan softens slightly but doesn’t fully melt into a sauce.
  5. 5 Transfer the asparagus to a serving platter. Taste and adjust seasoning with a pinch more salt or a squeeze of fresh lemon juice if you want extra brightness. Serve warm alongside your main dishes. 2 minutes Dish is vibrant, fresh, and ready to eat.

item.title: Sweet Easter Basket Brownies Kids Can Make and Love
item.description: These Easter basket brownies are a fun, simple treat that kids can help make. The secret? Mini candy eggs tucked inside for a surprise crunch. Perfect for springtime celebrations or an easy holiday dessert! | ⏱️ Prep: 20min | Cook: 25min | Serves: 12 | #cat1761230294638 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 20 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 25 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 45 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 12

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 cup unsalted butter, melted
  • 2 cups granulated sugar
  • 4 large eggs
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 3/4 cup unsweetened cocoa powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
  • 1 cup mini chocolate chips
  • 1 cup mini candy-coated chocolate eggs

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease a 9x13-inch baking pan with butter or line it with parchment paper. This step ensures your brownies won’t stick and makes cleanup easier. It’s important to have the oven fully heated before you bake so the brownies cook evenly without drying out. If your oven runs hot or cool, an oven thermometer can help you maintain the right temperature for the perfect fudgy texture. 10 minutes Oven preheated to 350°F and pan properly greased or lined
  2. 2 In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the melted butter and granulated sugar until smooth and glossy. This helps dissolve the sugar and creates a shiny batter that bakes into a moist brownie. Add the eggs one at a time, whisking well after each addition to incorporate air and build structure. Stir in the vanilla extract for flavor depth. Avoid rushing here as beating too vigorously can make the brownies cakier instead of fudgy. 5 minutes Batter is smooth, thick, and glossy with no lumps
  3. 3 Sift together the flour, cocoa powder, salt, and baking powder in a separate bowl. Sifting helps break up clumps and aerates the dry ingredients for a lighter texture. Slowly fold the dry mix into the wet ingredients using a spatula. Folding gently is key here to avoid overmixing, which can toughen brownies. Stop mixing once you see no streaks of flour remaining to keep the batter tender and fudgy. 5 minutes Batter is thick and evenly combined without dry patches
  4. 4 Fold in the mini chocolate chips carefully to distribute them without crushing. This adds pockets of melted chocolate throughout the brownies. Then, gently fold in the mini candy-coated eggs last. Adding these at the end prevents them from melting into the batter, preserving their crunch and festive look. If you mix too vigorously, the candy shells may crack or bleed color. 3 minutes Candy eggs and chips are evenly dispersed and intact
  5. 5 Pour the batter evenly into your prepared pan and smooth the top with a spatula. Even spreading ensures uniform baking and a flat surface for cutting. Resist the urge to tap the pan excessively; a gentle shake is enough to settle the batter and release any air bubbles. This helps the brownies bake evenly and prevents holes or cracks on top. 3 minutes Batter is level and fills the pan evenly
  6. 6 Bake the brownies in the preheated oven for 22-25 minutes. Start checking at 22 minutes by inserting a toothpick into the center. You want a few moist crumbs to cling to the toothpick but no raw batter. Overbaking dries out the brownies and dulls the candy’s texture. Underbaking can leave them too gooey to cut cleanly. The edges will look set and slightly pulling away from the pan when done. 22-25 minutes Toothpick with moist crumbs and set edges
  7. 7 Remove the brownies from the oven and let them cool completely in the pan on a wire rack. Cooling is crucial because the brownies continue to set as they cool, making them easier to slice without crumbling. If you cut them too soon, they can fall apart. Patience here pays off with clean edges and a fudgy center. 30-40 minutes Brownies are firm to the touch and no longer warm
  8. 8 Once cooled, use a sharp knife to cut the brownies into 12 squares. Wiping the knife clean between cuts helps keep the edges neat. If you want extra festive presentation, sprinkle a few mini candy eggs on top before serving. These brownies hold their shape well but still have that classic chewy, fudgy bite that everyone loves. 10 minutes Clean, even brownie squares with visible candy pieces
  9. 9 Serve the brownies slightly chilled or at room temperature. They pair wonderfully with a glass of cold milk or a scoop of vanilla ice cream for an extra indulgent treat. Store leftovers in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days or refrigerate to keep the candy crisp longer. Immediate serving or storage Brownies retain moisture and candy crunch when served

item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 45 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 cup sourdough discard, room temperature
  • 2 1/2 cups all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting
  • 1 teaspoon salt
  • 1 teaspoon sugar
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil, plus extra for greasing
  • 3/4 cup warm water (about 110°F)
  • 1 teaspoon instant yeast

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Start by mixing the warm water with the instant yeast and sugar in a large bowl. Let it sit for about 5 minutes until it becomes frothy. This step activates the yeast, ensuring your dough will rise well. If you skip this or use water that’s too hot or cold, the yeast might not activate properly, resulting in dense dough. Watch for a bubbly surface—that’s your sign the yeast is alive and kicking. 5 minutes Frothy, bubbly mixture on top
  2. 2 Add the sourdough discard, olive oil, and salt to the yeast mixture. Stir to combine. Then gradually add the flour, mixing with a wooden spoon or your hands until it forms a shaggy dough. This gradual incorporation helps hydrate the flour evenly and prevents lumps. Rushing this step can make the dough uneven and harder to knead. 5 minutes Dough starts to come together but still a bit rough
  3. 3 Turn the dough onto a floured surface and knead for about 8-10 minutes. Use the heel of your hand to push the dough away, fold it back over, and repeat. This kneading develops gluten, which gives the dough structure and chewiness. If you don’t knead enough, the dough will be sticky and won’t rise properly; too much kneading can make it tough. You want a smooth, elastic dough that springs back when poked. 8-10 minutes Smooth, elastic dough that holds shape
  4. 4 Lightly oil a clean bowl and place your dough inside, turning it once to coat with oil. Cover with a damp cloth or plastic wrap and let it rise in a warm spot for about 45 minutes to an hour until it doubles in size. This resting period allows yeast to produce gas, making the dough airy. If your kitchen is cold, it might take longer, so be patient. Avoid rushing this step or the crust will be dense. 45-60 minutes Dough roughly doubled and soft to touch
  5. 5 Once risen, punch down the dough gently to release excess gas. Divide it into two equal portions for two pizzas. Shape each into a tight ball by folding edges underneath. This shaping helps create surface tension for a better rise in the oven. Don’t skip this or your dough may spread too much while baking. 5 minutes Smooth, tight dough balls with taut surface
  6. 6 Preheat your oven to 475°F (245°C) and if you have a pizza stone, place it inside to heat. This high heat is crucial for a crisp crust and proper oven spring. If you don’t have a stone, use a baking sheet turned upside down. Avoid lower temperatures which can make the crust soggy or pale. 15-20 minutes Oven fully heated and stone or tray very hot
  7. 7 On a floured surface, stretch or roll each dough ball into a 10-12 inch circle. Try not to overwork the dough here to keep it airy. If it resists stretching, let it rest for a few minutes to relax the gluten. This step shapes your pizza base and affects crust thickness and texture. 5-7 minutes Evenly stretched dough circles without holes
  8. 8 Transfer the dough onto a pizza peel or parchment paper. Add your favorite sauce, cheese, and toppings but don’t overload it or the crust won’t crisp properly. Less is more when it comes to toppings on homemade pizza dough. 5 minutes Evenly topped dough ready for oven
  9. 9 Slide the pizza onto the hot stone or baking sheet and bake for 10-15 minutes until the crust is golden and cheese is bubbly and slightly browned. Keep an eye on it so it doesn’t burn. The high heat develops a beautiful crust with those signature charred spots and a chewy interior. 10-15 minutes Golden crust with bubbly, browned cheese

item.title: No-Waste Sourdough Discard Banana Bread That’s Moist and Flavorful
item.description: This banana bread uses tangy sourdough discard to boost flavor and reduce waste. It’s super moist with a hint of sourdough tang, perfect for breakfast or afternoon snack. I love how it turns out every time! | ⏱️ Prep: 20min | Cook: 60min | Serves: 8 | #cat1761230294638 #Mediumpost #post #cooking #foodie
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 20 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 60 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 80 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 8

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 cup sourdough discard (unfed, room temperature)
  • 2 large ripe bananas, mashed
  • 1/2 cup granulated sugar
  • 1/4 cup brown sugar, packed
  • 1/3 cup vegetable oil
  • 2 large eggs, room temperature
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 teaspoon baking soda
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon
  • 1/2 cup chopped walnuts (optional)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease a 9x5-inch loaf pan with butter or nonstick spray. This ensures your banana bread won’t stick and will come out cleanly after baking. Also, line the pan with parchment paper if you want extra insurance. Proper oven temperature is key to baking evenly — too hot and the edges burn before the center cooks through, too cool and you get a dense, underbaked loaf. 10 minutes Loaf pan is evenly greased or lined, oven fully preheated
  2. 2 In a large bowl, whisk together the sourdough discard, mashed bananas, granulated sugar, brown sugar, vegetable oil, eggs, and vanilla extract until smooth and well combined. The sourdough discard adds moisture and a subtle tang that enhances the banana flavor. Mixing these wet ingredients thoroughly helps create a consistent batter, but be careful not to overmix later with the flour to avoid toughness. 5 minutes Mixture is smooth, homogenous, and slightly thick
  3. 3 In a separate bowl, sift together the all-purpose flour, baking soda, salt, and ground cinnamon. Sifting helps remove lumps and evenly distributes the leavening agents and spices, which is important for consistent rising and flavor throughout the loaf. 5 minutes Dry ingredients are well combined and lump-free
  4. 4 Gradually fold the dry ingredients into the wet mixture using a spatula. Fold gently to combine just until no streaks of flour remain. Overmixing at this stage can develop gluten and make the bread tough and chewy, so patience here is key for a tender crumb. 3 minutes Batter is smooth but still thick, no dry flour visible
  5. 5 If using, fold in the chopped walnuts now. They add a nice crunch and contrast to the moist bread. You can also substitute with pecans or leave nuts out if preferred. Make sure to fold evenly so nuts are distributed throughout without overworking the batter. 2 minutes Nuts are evenly dispersed in the batter
  6. 6 Pour the batter into the prepared loaf pan and smooth the top with a spatula. Smoothing helps the bread bake evenly and rise uniformly. Avoid tapping the pan too hard as it can knock out air bubbles that help keep the bread light. 2 minutes Batter evenly spread, surface level and smooth
  7. 7 Bake in the preheated oven for 55-65 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean or with just a few moist crumbs. Oven times vary, so start checking at 55 minutes. Baking too long dries out the bread, too short leaves it undercooked and gummy inside. 55-65 minutes Golden brown top, toothpick test passes
  8. 8 Remove the loaf from the oven and let it cool in the pan for 10 minutes. This resting time allows the structure to set so the bread doesn’t crumble when removed. Then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely before slicing to avoid a gummy texture. 10 minutes plus cooling Loaf slightly firm to touch, easy to remove from pan
  9. 9 Slice and serve! This banana bread is delicious on its own or with a smear of butter or cream cheese. It keeps well at room temperature for a couple of days if wrapped tightly, or you can freeze slices for longer storage. Immediate serving Slices hold shape, moist crumb with balanced texture

item.title: Crispy Sourdough Discard Focaccia Bread That’s So Easy to Make at Home
item.description: This focaccia uses sourdough discard to create a crispy, flavorful crust with a tender crumb inside. The secret? A generous olive oil drizzle and a quick overnight rise. Perfect for weekend brunch or alongside dinner. | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 30min | Serves: 6 | #cat1761230294638 #Mediumpost #1hourmeals #post #cooking
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 45 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 cup sourdough discard, room temperature
  • 2 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp instant yeast
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 cup warm water (about 80°F)
  • 1/4 cup extra virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling
  • Coarse sea salt, for topping
  • Fresh rosemary sprigs, for topping

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 In a large mixing bowl, combine the sourdough discard, warm water, and instant yeast. Stir gently to dissolve the yeast. This step activates the yeast and helps ensure your dough will rise well, even with the discard’s natural acidity. Avoid using water that’s too hot, as it can kill the yeast. Let this mixture sit for about 5 minutes until you see some bubbles forming, signaling that the yeast is alive and ready. 5 minutes Bubbles or foam forming on the surface of the mixture
  2. 2 Add the flour and salt to the yeast mixture. Stir with a wooden spoon or your hand until a rough dough forms. The dough will be sticky and shaggy at this point, which is exactly what you want for focaccia. Resist the temptation to add too much flour here. The stickiness helps create the airy texture inside the bread. Once combined, cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp towel to prevent drying out. 5 minutes No dry flour visible and dough holding together loosely
  3. 3 Let the dough rest at room temperature for 1 hour. This bulk fermentation allows the yeast to start working and the gluten to develop. You’ll notice the dough puffing up slightly and becoming smoother. During this rest, you can perform a quick stretch and fold once to strengthen the dough, which helps build structure without kneading. 60 minutes Dough has risen slightly and surface looks smoother
  4. 4 Transfer the dough to a well-oiled 9x13 inch baking pan. Using your oiled fingers, gently press and stretch the dough to fill the pan. Don’t worry if it resists a bit; let it rest for a few minutes and then stretch more. This shaping step is key to getting focaccia’s signature flat but airy texture. Cover the pan with plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight or for at least 8 hours to develop flavor and texture. 10 minutes plus overnight rest Dough fills the pan with some elasticity and slight bubbles visible
  5. 5 Remove the dough from the fridge about 30 minutes before baking to come to room temperature. Preheat your oven to 450°F (230°C). This high temperature is important for crisping the crust quickly. While waiting, drizzle the dough generously with olive oil, then use your fingertips to dimple the surface deeply. These dimples trap the oil and create the focaccia’s characteristic texture. 30 minutes Surface of dough is oily and deeply dimpled
  6. 6 Sprinkle coarse sea salt and fresh rosemary sprigs evenly over the oiled dough. The salt adds bursts of flavor and crunch, while rosemary infuses an aromatic note. Don’t be shy with these toppings; they’re what make the focaccia special. If you want a different flavor, try thyme or olives instead. 5 minutes Even coverage of salt crystals and herbs on dough surface
  7. 7 Bake the focaccia in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes. Keep an eye on the color; you want a deep golden brown crust with some darker spots where the oil has caramelized. This caramelization is what gives focaccia its irresistible crispy edges and rich flavor. Avoid opening the oven door too often, as it can cause the bread to collapse. 25-30 minutes Golden brown crust with bubbly, blistered surface
  8. 8 Once baked, remove the focaccia from the oven and immediately drizzle with a little more olive oil while it’s hot. This final touch keeps the crust moist and shiny. Let it cool in the pan for about 10 minutes before slicing. Cooling helps the crumb set and prevents it from becoming gummy when cut. 10 minutes Focaccia is slightly warm and shiny with a crispy crust
  9. 9 Slice the focaccia into squares and serve warm or at room temperature. It’s fantastic on its own, with soups, or used for sandwiches. Leftovers can be toasted the next day to refresh the crust. Enjoy the contrast of the crunchy outside and pillowy soft inside! 0 minutes Clean slices revealing airy crumb and golden crust

item.description: These sourdough discard cookies are a fun way to use up starter and whip up a sweet treat. They’re soft with a hint of tang, perfect for after school or a cozy snack. Plus, no fancy ingredients needed! | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 12min | Serves: 24 | #cat1761230294638 #Easypost #quickpost #30minutemeals #post
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 12 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 27 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 24

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 cup sourdough starter discard (unfed)
  • 1/2 cup unsalted butter, softened
  • 3/4 cup brown sugar, packed
  • 1 large egg
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
  • 1/4 teaspoon salt
  • 1 cup chocolate chips

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Start by creaming the softened butter and brown sugar together in a large bowl using a hand mixer or stand mixer. This step is crucial because creaming incorporates air into the mixture, giving your cookies a light and tender texture. Beat them together until the mixture is fluffy and pale, about 2-3 minutes. Avoid skipping this or rushing it, as under-creamed butter can lead to dense cookies. 3 minutes Mixture looks light in color and fluffy in texture
  2. 2 Add the egg and vanilla extract to the creamed butter and sugar, then mix until combined. This step helps bind the ingredients and adds moisture. Be sure to scrape down the bowl to mix everything evenly. If you mix too aggressively here, the dough might become tough, so just mix until no streaks remain. 1-2 minutes Smooth, homogenous batter without visible egg streaks
  3. 3 In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, and salt. This ensures even distribution of the leavening agent and salt, which helps the cookies rise properly and enhances flavor. Skipping this step can cause uneven texture or salty pockets in your cookies. 2 minutes Dry ingredients fully combined, no clumps
  4. 4 Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture, mixing on low speed just until combined. Overmixing at this stage develops gluten, which can make cookies tough instead of soft and chewy. Stop as soon as you see no more flour. 2-3 minutes Dough comes together but still soft and slightly sticky
  5. 5 Fold in the sourdough starter discard gently with a spatula. The discard adds moisture and a subtle tang, contributing to the cookie’s unique flavor and soft texture. Mixing too vigorously here can deflate the dough and affect texture, so fold carefully. 1 minute Starter evenly incorporated, dough smooth
  6. 6 Finally, fold in the chocolate chips evenly. This distributes the chocolate throughout the dough so every bite has a melty surprise. Be gentle to keep the dough’s texture intact. 1 minute Chocolate chips evenly spread without overmixing dough
  7. 7 Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper. This prevents sticking and promotes even baking. Make sure the oven is fully preheated before baking for best results. 10 minutes (oven preheat) Oven temperature stabilized at 350°F
  8. 8 Scoop dough by rounded tablespoons onto the baking sheet, spacing about 2 inches apart to allow spreading. This spacing helps cookies bake evenly without merging into each other. 5 minutes Evenly spaced dough mounds on baking sheet
  9. 9 Bake for 10-12 minutes until the edges are golden but the centers still look slightly soft. This ensures a chewy center with crisp edges. Overbaking will dry them out, so watch closely in the last few minutes. 10-12 minutes Edges golden brown, centers set but soft to touch
  10. 10 Remove from oven and let cookies cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack. Cooling on the sheet allows them to firm up without breaking apart. 5 minutes Cookies set enough to move without crumbling

item.title: Simple and Crispy 5-Ingredient Sourdough Discard Crackers You’ll Love
item.description: These crunchy sourdough discard crackers are a lifesaver for using up starter leftovers. Just five ingredients, super easy to make, and perfect for snacking or pairing with cheese. You’ll love how crispy and flavorful they turn out! | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 20min | Serves: 8 | #cat1761230221583 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking

item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 20 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 35 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 8

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 cup sourdough starter discard, unfed and room temperature
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1/4 cup olive oil
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1 teaspoon dried herbs (like rosemary or thyme), optional

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 In a large mixing bowl, combine the sourdough starter discard, all-purpose flour, olive oil, salt, and dried herbs if using. Stir everything together until a shaggy dough forms. It might be a little sticky, but that’s okay. The key here is to mix just until combined to avoid overworking the dough, which can make the crackers tough. Using your hands to bring the dough together helps you feel when it’s ready. Avoid adding extra flour at this stage because the dough should be pliable but not dry. 5 minutes Dough holds together in a rough ball, slightly tacky but not overly sticky
  2. 2 Lightly flour a clean surface and roll the dough out very thin, about 1/8 inch thick or thinner if you want crispier crackers. The thinner you roll, the crispier the final crackers will be. Use a sharp knife or pizza cutter to score the dough into squares or rectangles before baking. This pre-scoring makes it easy to break the crackers apart after baking without cracking them unevenly. If you skip this, you might end up with irregular shapes or broken crackers. 7 minutes Dough is evenly rolled thin with clear scored lines for easy breaking
  3. 3 Transfer the rolled and scored dough onto a parchment-lined baking sheet. Prick each cracker a few times with a fork to prevent air bubbles from forming during baking. This step ensures the crackers bake flat and crisp rather than puffing up. If you skip pricking, you might get uneven textures and bubbles, which can make the crackers less enjoyable. 3 minutes Evenly spaced fork holes visible on each cracker piece
  4. 4 Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Place the baking sheet with the dough in the oven and bake for about 18-20 minutes. Keep an eye on them starting at 15 minutes because ovens vary. You want the crackers to turn a nice golden brown without burning. If you bake too long, they’ll taste bitter; too short, and they’ll be soft. 18-20 minutes Crackers are golden brown and crisp to the touch
  5. 5 Once baked, remove the crackers from the oven and let them cool completely on the baking sheet. Cooling is important because the crackers will crisp up further as they cool. Don’t try to break them apart while warm or they might be soft and crumbly. Patience here really pays off for that perfect crunch. 15 minutes Crackers are firm and snap easily when broken
  6. 6 Carefully break the crackers along the scored lines. If any edges are uneven, you can trim them off or save the scraps for snacking. Store the crackers in an airtight container once fully cooled to keep them crisp. Moisture is the enemy here, so airtight storage is key. 5 minutes Clean breaks along scored lines with minimal crumbs
  7. 7 For extra flavor, sprinkle a little flaky sea salt or your favorite spice blend on top right after baking while the crackers are still warm. This adds a nice finishing touch and elevates the taste. Don’t add salt before baking or it can draw out moisture and affect texture. 2 minutes Even coating of seasoning on crackers without sogginess
  8. 8 Serve these crackers as a snack on their own or with cheese, hummus, or your favorite dip. They’re sturdy enough to hold toppings without crumbling, making them perfect for entertaining or a simple afternoon nibble. Enjoy the tangy flavor from the sourdough and the satisfying crunch with every bite. Immediate Crackers hold toppings without breaking
  9. 9 If you want to store leftovers, keep them in an airtight container at room temperature. Avoid refrigeration as it can introduce moisture and make crackers soft. When ready to eat again, give them a quick toast in the oven at 300°F for 3-5 minutes to refresh the crispiness. N/A Crackers regain crunch after reheating

item.title: Fluffy Sourdough Discard Waffles Perfect for Weekend Mornings
item.description: These waffles use sourdough discard to add a subtle tang and extra fluffiness. They’re a fun way to reduce waste and make weekend breakfasts feel special. Crispy outside, tender inside — perfect with maple syrup! | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 10min | Serves: 4 | #cat1761230422512 #Easypost #quickpost #30minutemeals #post
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 10 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 25 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 cup sourdough starter discard, unfed and bubbly
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1 tablespoon sugar
  • 1 teaspoon baking powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
  • 1/4 teaspoon salt
  • 1 large egg
  • 3/4 cup milk
  • 3 tablespoons melted butter, cooled
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 In a large bowl, whisk together the sourdough discard, milk, egg, melted butter, and vanilla extract. Combining the wet ingredients first helps create a smooth base for the batter and ensures the butter is evenly distributed, which contributes to the waffles’ tender texture. Make sure the melted butter isn’t too hot or it could cook the egg prematurely. Mixing gently but thoroughly at this stage sets you up for a light batter. 5 minutes Smooth, slightly thick batter with no lumps of egg or butter visible
  2. 2 In a separate bowl, sift together the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Sifting helps aerate the dry ingredients and prevents clumps, which is crucial for fluffy waffles. The baking powder and baking soda work together to give the waffles their rise and lightness, so don’t skip or reduce these. Once sifted, gently fold the dry ingredients into the wet mixture until just combined. Overmixing can develop gluten and make the waffles tough, so it’s okay if a few lumps remain. 5 minutes Batter holds together but still has some small lumps; not overly smooth or runny
  3. 3 Let the batter rest at room temperature for 10 to 15 minutes. This resting period allows the baking powder and baking soda to activate fully and the gluten to relax, resulting in a lighter, fluffier waffle. It also gives the sourdough flavor a chance to mellow and develop. Resist the urge to skip this step — it really makes a difference in texture. 10-15 minutes Batter slightly thickens and bubbles start to form on the surface
  4. 4 Preheat your waffle iron according to the manufacturer’s instructions and lightly grease it with butter or non-stick spray. A well-heated waffle iron ensures the batter cooks quickly, producing a crisp exterior without drying out the inside. Greasing prevents sticking and helps achieve those beautiful golden edges. Avoid opening the iron too early during cooking to prevent tearing the waffle. 5 minutes Waffle iron is hot and ready; test with a drop of water that sizzles immediately
  5. 5 Pour about 1/3 to 1/2 cup of batter onto the center of the waffle iron, spreading it gently if needed. Close the lid and cook according to your waffle maker’s instructions, usually 3-5 minutes. Cooking time varies by model, so watch for steam to slow down, which signals the waffle is done. Resist the temptation to peek too early, as this can cause the waffle to stick or tear. 3-5 minutes Steam reduces significantly and waffle surface looks golden brown
  6. 6 Carefully remove the waffle using a non-metallic utensil to avoid scratching the iron. Place it on a wire rack instead of stacking to keep it crisp. Stacking waffles traps steam and makes them soggy, which defeats the purpose of that perfect crispy exterior. If making multiple waffles, keep finished ones warm in a low oven (around 200°F) on the rack. 2 minutes Waffle lifts easily and holds shape without breaking
  7. 7 Repeat the cooking process with the remaining batter, greasing the waffle iron as needed between batches. Consistent greasing prevents sticking and helps maintain the waffle iron's non-stick surface. If the batter thickens too much while cooking, stir gently to loosen it before pouring. 10-15 minutes Each waffle cooks evenly golden brown and crisps properly
  8. 8 Serve the waffles immediately with your favorite toppings like maple syrup, fresh fruit, or a dollop of whipped cream. These waffles are best enjoyed fresh to preserve their crispness and fluffy interior. If you need to keep them longer, see storage tips below. Immediate Warm, crisp waffles with tender, airy crumb inside
  9. 9 Clean your waffle iron after it cools by wiping with a damp cloth. Avoid immersing in water to protect the heating elements. Proper maintenance keeps your waffle iron working well for many breakfasts to come. 5 minutes Waffle iron surface clean and dry

item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 10 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 5 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 15 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 cup sourdough starter discard (unfed)
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1 tablespoon sugar
  • 1 teaspoon baking powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1 cup milk (whole or 2%)
  • 1 large egg
  • 2 tablespoons melted butter or oil
  • Butter or oil for cooking

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 In a large bowl, whisk together the sourdough discard, milk, egg, and melted butter until smooth. This step is important because it blends the wet ingredients thoroughly, ensuring even hydration of the flour later. If you skip properly mixing here, you risk uneven batter with lumps or dry spots. The discard’s acidity will also start reacting with the baking soda once combined, so mixing well helps activate that leavening. 3 minutes The mixture should be smooth and slightly thick but pourable, with no streaks of egg or butter.
  2. 2 In a separate bowl, combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Stir these dry ingredients well to evenly distribute the leavening agents and salt. This ensures your pancakes rise uniformly and have balanced flavor. If you rush this or don’t mix enough, you might end up with pockets of baking powder or salt, causing uneven taste or texture. 2 minutes The dry mixture should look uniform with no clumps or uneven patches.
  3. 3 Gently fold the dry ingredients into the wet mixture with a spatula. It’s key not to overmix here; stir just until you don’t see flour anymore. Overmixing develops gluten, which can make pancakes tough instead of tender and fluffy. You want a slightly lumpy batter for the best texture. 2 minutes Batter should be thick but still pourable, with small lumps visible.
  4. 4 Let the batter rest at room temperature for about 5 minutes. This rest time allows the baking soda and sourdough acidity to react fully, creating bubbles that help the pancakes rise. It also hydrates the flour, making the final texture lighter and more tender. Skipping this step usually leads to flatter pancakes. 5 minutes You’ll notice tiny bubbles forming on the surface of the batter.
  5. 5 Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat and lightly grease with butter or oil. The pan needs to be hot enough so the batter sizzles gently when poured but not so hot it burns. Too low heat means flat, rubbery pancakes; too high burns the outside while leaving the inside raw. 3 minutes When a few drops of water dance and evaporate on the surface, the pan is ready.
  6. 6 Pour 1/4 cup of batter per pancake onto the skillet, spacing them apart. Cook undisturbed until bubbles form on the surface and edges look set, about 2-3 minutes. This bubbling is a sign the leavening is working and the pancake is cooking through. Resist flipping too early or the pancake won’t be fully cooked inside. 3 minutes Bubbles appear on top and edges look dry and slightly firm.
  7. 7 Flip the pancakes gently with a spatula and cook the other side for 1-2 minutes until golden brown. The second side cooks faster, so keep an eye to avoid burning. The pancake should spring back slightly when pressed and have a golden crust. 2 minutes Golden brown color on the flipped side and a fluffy interior texture.
  8. 8 Transfer cooked pancakes to a warm plate and cover loosely with foil to keep warm while you finish the rest. This prevents them from drying out or getting cold. If you stack them without covering, steam can make them soggy. 1 minute Pancakes remain warm and soft without becoming soggy.
  9. 9 Serve your sourdough discard pancakes warm with maple syrup, fresh berries, or your favorite toppings. They’re best eaten fresh but also reheat well if needed. Enjoy the subtle tang and fluffy texture that make these pancakes a delightful twist on a classic breakfast. Immediate Pancakes are warm, fluffy, and slightly tangy with a golden crust.

media:content.media:title: Fluffy Sourdough Discard Pancakes Ready in 15 Minutes
media:content.media:description: These pancakes use sourdough discard for a tangy twist and extra fluffiness. Super quick to whip up, perfect for lazy weekend breakfasts or brunch with friends.
